The auditor server's GET /nav endpoint produces compatible payloads."}},"notice":"Fetches auditor-signed NAV updates from an off-chain API via         Chainlink Functions and pushes them to FundVault. FLOW:   Chainlink Automation (hourly) \u2192 requestNAVUpdate()     \u2192 Chainlink Functions DON fetches {nav, timestamp, nonce, sig} from API     \u2192 fulfillRequest() verifies EIP-712 sig from registered auditor     \u2192 calls vault.updateNAV(nav) REPLAY PROTECTION:   - nonce: monotonic counter, must be > lastNonce   - timestamp: must be newer than last NAV update AND within MAX_PAYLOAD_AGE   - vault: bound in EIP-712 struct \u2014 sig for vault A cannot be used on vault B EMERGENCY:   If Chainlink is down, admin retains NAV_UPDATER_ROLE on FundVault and   can call vault.updateNAV() eip712Domain().details: returns the fields and values that describe the domain separator used by this contract for EIP-712 signature.

handleOracleFulfillment(bytes32,bytes,by.details: Either response or error parameter will be set, but never both.

params.err: Aggregated error either from the request's source code or from the execution pipeline.
params.response: Aggregated response from the request's source code.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

checkUpkeep(bytes).notice: Called by Chainlink Automation nodes to check if upkeep is needed.         Returns true once per requestInterval to prevent double-fire.

handleOracleFulfillment(bytes32,bytes,by.notice: Chainlink Functions response handler called by the Functions Router during fullilment from the designated transmitter node in an OCR round.

requestNAVUpdate().notice: Anyone can trigger a NAV request (permissionless — sig is the auth).         Respects the cooldown to avoid duplicate in-flight requests.

submitSignedNAV(uint256,uint256,uint256,.notice: Submit a pre-signed NAV update directly, without going through         Chainlink Functions. Useful for demos and emergency manual updates.         Anyone can call this — the EIP-712 signature is the only auth.         The auditor server's GET /nav endpoint produces compatible payloads.
